violations.text: Inspector observed grease debris on lid of grease receptacle. New Violation. Correct By: 01/02/2026
The floors in various areas are in poor repair (exposed cement needs resealing). The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 05/31/2026
Management's plan for assessing, monitoring, and controlling foodborne disease hazards is inadequate. New Violation. Correct By: 12/03/2025

An open employees' beverage container was observed stored in a meat preparation area. Employees shall not drink from open beverage containers in order to prevent contamination of their hands, the container, and exposed food. PIC disposed of beverage and talked with staff. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/03/2025

Soap was not available at the hand-washing sink in the seafood, 3 bay sink, and meat cutting areas. Hand-washing sinks must be provided with a supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder, or bar soap. PIC placed soap during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/03/2025

The hand-washing sink in the 3 bay sink area was blocked by a coffee maker. A hand-washing sink shall be accessible at all times for employee use. PIC removed coffee maker. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/03/2025
The handwashing sink in the meat cutting area is missing the proper handwashing signage. New Violation. Correct By: 12/03/2025

Raw animal foods (such as raw eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (such as produce and milk) in the walk-in refrigerator and lowboy cooler. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. PIC relocated eggs. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/03/2025

The quaternary ammonium concentration in the sanitizing solution of the meat cutting 3-bay sink was 100 parts per million (ppm). According to the manufacturer's use directions, the concentration of the quaternary ammonium must be between 200-400 ppm. PIC refilled sink and retested. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/03/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (knives) in the kitchen area were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. PIC placed in 3 bay sink to be washed/sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/03/2025

Per interview of staff, deli slicers are only wiped down with water and a rag after every use. The surfaces of equipment that contacts time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as meats and cheeses must be washed, rinsed, and sanitized at least every four hours. PIC took units out of service to be washed/sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/03/2025

Bacalao was observed held at 51 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC placed in cooler for active cooling. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/03/2025

Quaternary ammonium sanitizer solution was in excess of 400 parts per million (ppm) in the kitchen area. A quaternary ammonia sanitizer solution applied to food contact surfaces must be applied at the strength as indicated on the manufacturers' label. PIC emptied sink, refilled, and retested. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/03/2025

Inspector observed previously frozen ROP fish held under refrigeration in the seafood display unit. ROP fish that bears a label indicating that it is to be kept frozen until time of use shall be removed from the reduced oxygen environment prior to its thawing under refrigeration. PIC removed fish from ROP packaging. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/03/2025

The door to the outside, located in the back storage area, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 12/17/2025
Inspector observed fly traps with heavy accumulation of flies in the back storage hallway. New Violation. Correct By: 12/03/2025

Inspector observed knives and spatulas being stored in-between wall and 3 bay sink. PIC removed items and placed in sink to be washed/sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/03/2025
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the quat sanitizing solution for the 3 bay sinks. New Violation. Correct By: 12/03/2025
The outside grease receptacle was observed in poor repair (lid was broken). New Violation. Correct By: 12/03/2025
Inspector observed a heavy trash, leaf, and other debris around refuse area. Area to be maintained clean. New Violation. Correct By: 12/03/2025
The floors in various areas are in poor repair (exposed cement need resealing. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 05/31/2026

The following were observed in poor repair. - seafood walk-in door (door doesn't fully shut) - seafood walk-in cooler condenser (heavy ice build up) - meat walk-in cooler condenser (water dripping) - wall near mop sink area (hole) - deli open case coolers (non-functioning - no TCS items observed)The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. PIC has workorder for units and repair company was on site during inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 12/17/2025

The walls in the seafood 3 bay sink area has an accumulation of soil residue and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 12/03/2025

Packaged Deli foods in the food establishment are lacking all required information on labels. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. Foods were removed from self-service sale and labels are being made. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/18/2023
